```{r, message = F, warning = F}
if (!requireNamespace("pacman", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("pacman")
}
library(pacman)

# Load necessary libraries
p_load(caret)
p_load(tidyverse)
p_load(tidymodels)
p_load(tidytext)
p_load(textrecipes)
p_load(Matrix)
p_load(wordcloud)
p_load(tm)

p_load(stopwords)
p_load(RColorBrewer)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Data Preparation
# ---------------------------------------------------------

df <- read_csv("../../data/processed/glassdoor_reviews_cleaned.csv")

cols <- c('summary', 'advice_to_management', 'review_pros', 'review_cons')
encode_cols <- c('covid_related_review', 'flags_business_outlook', 'flags_ceo_approval',
                 'flags_recommend_frend', 'rating_culture_values',
                 'rating_diversity_inclusion', 'rating_overall', 'rating_work_life',
                 'rating_compensation_benefits', 'rating_senior_leadership',
                 'rating_career_opportunities',
                 'has_review_text')
df <- df %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols), ~replace_na(., "")))
# Define the columns



# 1. Define custom stopwords
# Equivalent to STOPWORDS.union(set([...]))
custom_stopwords <- c(stopwords("english"), 
                      "bad", "good", "great", "will", "company", 
                      "job", "work", "employee", "here", 
                      "people", "employees")

# 2. Define the plotting function
plotWordCloudFromDF <- function(df, column_name) {
  
  # Extract text and collapse into one large string
  text_data <- paste(df[[column_name]], collapse = " ")
  
  # Create a Corpus and clean the text
  docs <- Corpus(VectorSource(text_data))
  docs <- docs %>%
    tm_map(content_transformer(tolower)) %>%
    tm_map(removePunctuation) %>%
    tm_map(removeNumbers) %>%
    tm_map(removeWords, custom_stopwords) %>%
    tm_map(stripWhitespace)
  
  # Generate the Word Cloud
  cat(paste0("Generating word cloud for: ", column_name, "\n"))
  wordcloud(words = docs, 
            max.words = 300, 
            random.order = FALSE, 
            rot.per = 0.35,            # Rotation percentage
            colors = brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"),
            scale = c(3, 0.5))        # Range of font sizes
}

# 3. Loop through columns

for (col in cols) {
  plotWordCloudFromDF(df, col)
}

```

```{r}
# Split Data (equivalent to train_test_split)
set.seed(42)
df2 = df %>%
  subset(select = -c(rating_date, employee_status, company_name, 
  employee_location, employee_job_title, sector,
  review_id))


# Build the Preprocessing Recipe (Equivalent to ColumnTransformer + TfidfVectorizer)
rec <- recipe(employee_length + employee_job_quit ~ ., data = df2)  %>%
  step_mutate(across(all_of(encode_cols), as.factor)) %>%
  # Handle Numeric/Categorical preprocessing
  step_novel(all_of(encode_cols)) %>% 
  step_dummy(all_of(encode_cols), one_hot = TRUE) %>%
  
  # Text Processing for 'summary' (max_features = 200)
  step_tokenize(summary) %>%
  step_stopwords(summary) %>%
  step_ngram(summary, min_num_tokens = 1, num_tokens = 3) %>%
  step_tokenfilter(summary, max_tokens = 200, min_times = 5) %>%
  step_tfidf(summary, prefix = "summary__") %>%
  
  # Text Processing for 'advice_to_management' (max_features = 200)
  step_tokenize(advice_to_management) %>%
  step_stopwords(advice_to_management) %>%
  step_ngram(advice_to_management, min_num_tokens = 1, num_tokens = 3) %>%
  step_tokenfilter(advice_to_management, max_tokens = 200, min_times = 5) %>%
  step_tfidf(advice_to_management, prefix = "advice__") %>%
  
  # Text Processing for 'review_pros' (max_features = 300)
  step_tokenize(review_pros) %>%
  step_stopwords(review_pros) %>%
  step_ngram(review_pros, min_num_tokens = 1, num_tokens = 3) %>%
  step_tokenfilter(review_pros, max_tokens = 300, min_times = 5) %>%
  step_tfidf(review_pros, prefix = "pros__") %>%
  
  # Text Processing for 'review_cons' (max_features = 400)
  step_tokenize(review_cons) %>%
  step_stopwords(review_cons) %>%
  step_ngram(review_cons, min_num_tokens = 1, num_tokens = 3) %>%
  step_tokenfilter(review_cons, max_tokens = 400, min_times = 5) %>%
  step_tfidf(review_cons, prefix = "cons__") %>%
  
  # Scaling (Equivalent to MaxAbsScaler)
  # R's step_normalize is Z-score; for MaxAbs we use a custom scaling or range
  step_range(all_predictors(), min = -1, max = 1)


set.seed(42)
data_split <- df2$employee_length%>%
  createDataPartition(p = 0.8, list = F)
train_data <- df2[data_split, ]
test_data  <- df2[-data_split, ]

# Train the recipe on training data
prep_rec <- prep(rec, training = train_data)

# Transform the datasets (Equivalent to fit_transform / transform)
X_train_processed <- bake(prep_rec, new_data = NULL, all_predictors(), composition = "dgCMatrix")
X_test_processed  <- bake(prep_rec, new_data = test_data, all_predictors(), composition = "dgCMatrix")

# Target vectors
yl_train <- train_data$employee_length
yq_train <- as.integer(train_data$employee_job_quit)

# --- Data Structural Audit ---
cat("--- Data Structural Audit ---\n")
cat(sprintf("Total Combined Features: %d\n", ncol(X_train_processed)))
cat(sprintf("Total Samples: %d\n", nrow(X_train_processed)))
cat("First 30 Feature Names (Sample):\n")
print(colnames(X_train_processed)[1:30])

```



```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidytext)

# clean function
clean_words <- function(data, column_name) {
  
  # 1. convert to tidy (Tokenization & Cleaning)
  # unnest_tokens will transfer to lowercase and delete non-letter chars
  tidy_text <- data %>%
    select(all_of(column_name)) %>%
    unnest_tokens(word, !!sym(column_name))
  
  # total words
  wa <- tidy_text %>% distinct(word) %>% nrow()
  
  # 2. filter stopwords and low frequency
  # custom_stopwords is defined before
  cleaned_text <- tidy_text %>%
    filter(!word %in% custom_stopwords) %>%
    group_by(word) %>%
    filter(n() >= 5) %>% # drop words less than 5 times
    ungroup()
  
  # word number after filter
  wb <- cleaned_text %>% distinct(word) %>% nrow()
  
  # print the statistics
  cat(sprintf("%s original words: %d  stopword and lowfreq removed: %d\n", 
              column_name, wa, wb))
  
  # 3. calculate the top 20
  word_counts <- cleaned_text %>%
    count(word, sort = TRUE) %>%
    slice_head(n = 20)
  
  # 4. visualization
  p <- ggplot(word_counts, aes(x = reorder(word, -n), y = n)) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
    labs(title = paste("Top Word Frequencies of", column_name),
         x = "Words", y = "Frequency") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  print(p)
  
  # return complete word frequency
  full_counts <- cleaned_text %>%
    count(word, sort = TRUE)
  
  return(full_counts)
}

results <- list()
for (col in cols) {
  results[[col]] <- clean_words(df, col)
}
```
